task: Add a premium governed agent workbench, auditable owner-notification console, and measurable weekly owner review to the Property Portal template.
audience: Independent property owners, boutique agencies, and implementation partners
first_read: Bounded agent work, owner escalation, and weekly operating outcomes are visible as durable evidence; no approval, acknowledgement, or review performs an external commitment.

references.label: Existing Property Portal control center
references.reason: Preserve the established operational density, authority language, status hierarchy, and responsive behavior.

direction.signature: Every workflow stage earns the next stage through durable receipts, while urgent delivery, fallback, and owner acknowledgement remain separately auditable.

asset_quality.why_premium: The product UI itself is the correct primary asset for this protected operational surface.

artifacts.notes: Playwright-core full-page capture at 1440x1200; compact navigation, runtime posture, stage rail, two-lane workflow, disabled state, and multiline controls inspected.
Playwright-core full-page capture at 390x844; intentional stack, readable controls, multiline metric, tap targets, and page width inspected.
Playwright-core full-page capture at 1440x1200; lifecycle posture, four-column metrics, urgent queue, proof hash, and acknowledgement action inspected.
Playwright-core full-page capture at 390x844; stable three-column navigation, one-column lifecycle stack, wrapped proof hash, tap targets, and page width inspected.
Playwright-core full-page capture at 1440x1200; measured weekly hierarchy, five observation states, Keep / Change / Stop evidence, and release context inspected.
Playwright-core full-page capture at 390x844; stable navigation, current-week completion state, one-column observations, decisions, and page width inspected.

checks.name: asset-tier
checks.evidence: Tier C product UI is the correct medium for an owner operations console.

checks.name: export-inspection
checks.evidence: Owned full-page workbench and notification captures were inspected at 1440px desktop and 390px mobile.

checks.name: visual-qa
checks.evidence: Root overflow, clipped text, navigation width, and menu viewport checks passed at both viewports; locked and not-configured states remain coherent.

checks.name: notification-authority
checks.evidence: The console distinguishes delivery, failure, fallback, and owner acknowledgement without presenting acknowledgement as authorization to contact a renter or vendor.

checks.name: weekly-evidence
checks.evidence: The console distinguishes met, not-met, and unmeasured evidence, locks the completed current-week state, and bounds zero-action proof to the governed product surface.

score.notes: Strong first read, operational hierarchy, evidence specificity, stable small-screen navigation, and explicit authority. Final points require connected-runtime and repeated owner usability evidence.

known_risks: Sample property media remains unsuitable for a real client launch.
Live generation depends on a separately deployed MCP runtime, database, and model provider configuration.
A local webhook proof is not live provider-delivery evidence; production acceptance still requires timestamped primary and fallback receipts.
Weekly review metrics have local deterministic proof but still require a live tenant-scoped Postgres review and repeated owner usability evidence.
